Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

(2 comments)

What about including asm/byteorder.h and using the __ prefixed versions in userspace.

Then we have consistent definitions and no strange issues later.

Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

I discovered if we use the uapi header byteorder.h that will require us to include the uapi directory from the linux development tree. I discovered this requires some surgery since uapi is not standardize with the current distros we support. I'm going to wait on this until RHEL6 and SLES11 are dropped from 2.9 support to avoid the path nightmare this will entail.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 5:

> It's a userspace file, so I think it should be using standard userspace APIs. If you prefer not to change it, that's ok too.

leXXtoh() is for uintXX_t.
__leXX_to_cpu() is for __uXX.

The underlying types of uint64_t and __u64 may or may not be the same. Since our structures are written using __uXX it follows that  __leXX_to_cpu() are the correct functions to use.

parents.subject: LU-8472 scrub: try to avoid recovery during OI scrub

commit.subject: LU-6245 libcfs: remove byteorder.h
commit.message:
LU-6245 libcfs: remove byteorder.h

With the cleanup of userland with libcfs we no longer
need the special byte ordering macros. Kernel space
can just use what is provided by the kernel already.

Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
